mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production Index over the prior three months reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when INDPRO increased over the prior three months, or the Rank IC is negative over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7.0% indicates persistent labor market slack that suppresses wage costs and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days until the threshold is breached downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low federal funds rate and rising consumer prices compresses real yields, forcing investors out of fixed income and into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are not significantly higher during joint low-FEDFUNDS and rising-CPIAUCSL periods compared to unconditional periods over a 3-year window, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a post-crisis recovery with elevated unemployment reflect a durable goods and construction rebound that boosts cyclical earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when HOUST rises while UNRATE stays above 6%, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is below zero over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An advancing retail sales figure reflects robust consumer expenditure and top-line revenue growth for corporations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS changes fail to coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC is not statistically different from zero over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 0.5% confirms aggregate demand momentum sufficient to sustain corporate profit expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered quarters where GDPC1 grows by more than 0.5%, or the trigger-based hit rate falls below 52% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production concurrent with a low federal funds rate indicates a supply-demand mismatch where capacity utilization improves without monetary restriction, lifting profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of high INDPRO and low FEDFUNDS does not produce positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls above 200,000 reflects accelerating employment income and aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS rises by more than 200,000, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.
